items.title: HoYoverse reveals Gamescom 2026 lineup
items.summary: HoYoverse brings Genshin Impact, Honkai: Star Rail, Zenless Zone Zero, and Gamescom debuts of Honkai: Nexus Anima and Petit Planet to Cologne, August 26-30.
items.content:
ⓘ Hoyoverse.com HoYoverse has confirmed its presence at Gamescom 2026. HoYoverse brings Genshin Impact, Honkai: Star Rail, Zenless Zone Zero, and Gamescom debuts of Honkai: Nexus Anima and Petit Planet to Cologne, August 26-30. Darryl Linington , Published 07/23/2026 🇩🇪 🇪🇸 ... 🇵🇹 🇮🇹 🇳🇱 🇫🇷 🇵🇱 🇹🇷 🇷🇺 🇭🇺 🇨🇳 🇸🇪 Gamescom Game Releases Gaming var loading_1880722 = false; var loading_num_1880722 = 0; function ajaxReload_1880722(tt_content_uid,page,items_per_page,no_url_change) { loading_1880722 = true; HoYoverse has confirmed its complete presence at Gamescom 2026, running August 26 to 30 in Cologne, Germany. The publisher will occupy roughly 660 square meters at Hall 6.1, Booth B030, with a separate business area appearance at Booth B051 in Hall 4.2, marking its first time exhibiting to media and industry partners at the show.

Two unreleased titles headline the booth. Honkai: Nexus Anima , a creature-collecting strategy game set in a town shared by humans and Anima, makes its Gamescom debut following a recent multi-platform "Evolution Test." Visitors will get hands-on access to Anima collecting, exploration, and Nexus Battles, as well as a large Umpurrella installation and limited-edition giveaways.

Petit Planet , HoYoverse's cozy galactic life sim, will hold its international exhibition debut under the theme "Connect the Vibe." Attendees can try a PC demo as a Planet Tender, farming and building alongside neighbors while exploring the Starsea. The booth includes meet-and-greets with racer Elsasani and appearances by Oumuamua, plus exclusive rewards for completing on-site activities.

Established titles round out the exhibition. Genshin Impact's area centers on the upcoming Snezhnaya region, recreating its railway system and Statue of The Seven, with merchandise tied to the hashtag #SnezhnayaGC2026. Honkai: Star Rail , fresh off its Version 4.4 update on July 15, brings a Planarcadia-themed space fantasy setup featuring a giant Phantasmoon display and appearances from Pom-Pom alongside cosplayers. Zenless Zone Zero , marking its second anniversary, offers an urban fantasy experience anchored by the Bangboo drone Eous, plus a vinyl listening session, cosplayer photo ops, runway shows, and mystery guest appearances.

HoYoverse credited Antom (Ant International) and UGREEN for booth construction support, with hardware supplied by NVIDIA GeForce RTX and iBUYPOWER. HoYoverse will share further details via its official channels and each title's respective social media pages ahead of the show.

items.title: Honkai: Star Rail Releases New Trailer for Part 2 of Fate/stay night Collaboration
HoYoverse Korea Unveils Genshin Impact and Honkai: Star Rail Special Themes to Celebrate New Samsung Galaxy Launch
items.summary: Source: HoYoverse ©HoYoverse HoYoverse Korea announced on the 23rd that it has partnered with Samsung Electronics to release two special themes commemorating the launch of the...
items.content:
HoYoverse Korea announced on the 23rd that it has partnered with Samsung Electronics to release two special themes commemorating the launch of the new Galaxy Z series, featuring the open-world RPG 'Genshin Impact' and the space fantasy RPG 'Honkai: Star Rail'.

This collaboration was designed to celebrate the launch of Samsung's next-generation flagship foldable phones—the Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, Fold8, and Flip8—allowing users to experience the characters and worlds of HoYoverse games in an immersive way on their Galaxy mobile screens.

The 'Genshin Impact' special theme features a mysterious journey guided by Lyney, complete with custom lock screens, wallpapers, and unique icons. It captures the character's bright and cheerful atmosphere, allowing users to experience the essence of 'Genshin Impact' in their daily lives.

The 'Honkai: Star Rail' special theme, titled 'Blade's Night,' centers on the character Blade. It incorporates graphic elements such as sword imagery and flame effects to capture the character's intense aura on the screen.

The two HoYoverse special themes will be available to customers in Korea who purchase and activate the new Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, Fold8, or Flip8. They can be downloaded from the special theme event page within the Galaxy Store from August 4 to October 31.

Offline events will also be held where users can experience the special themes. By visiting one of five designated Samsung Store locations—Hongdae, Daejeon, Sangmu, Shinsegae Daegu, or Shinsegae Centum City—customers can tag their Galaxy smartphones to an on-site NFC terminal to download the themes.

The offline event runs from July 23 to August 3. On the first day of the event, a limited quantity of 300 Galaxy Store discount coupons worth ₩20k will be distributed at each store

During the same period, experience events will also be held at 'Genshin Café in Seoul' and 'Genshin PC Lounge.' Visitors can try out the Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra and Fold8 on-site, download the Genshin Impact special theme, and participate in a Galaxy Store discount coupon event. The discount coupon event will take place for one day only, on July 23.

Meanwhile, the newly released Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra features a larger screen and an optimized display ratio, providing a comfortable gaming environment where users can enjoy the graphics and visual effects of Genshin Impact and Honkai: Star Rail with greater clarity.

Detailed information regarding each special theme and event can be found on the Galaxy Store event page and the official Naver Cafes for 'Genshin Impact' and 'Honkai: Star Rail'.

items.title: Honkai: Star Rail Teams Up with UGREEN for Charging Accessories, Including Limited Collector's Box
items.summary: Honkai: Star Rail is a popular Hoyoverse gacha game set in space, and fans have the chance to show off their love of the game with the UGREEN collection of charging accessories. This collection follows the successful Genshin Impact collaboration. The cream of the crop is the UGREEN x Honkai: Star Rail Limited Collectible Gift ... Read more
items.content:
Honkai: Star Rail is a popular Hoyoverse gacha game set in space, and fans have the chance to show off their love of the game with the UGREEN collection of charging accessories. This collection follows the successful Genshin Impact collaboration.

The cream of the crop is the UGREEN x Honkai: Star Rail Limited Collectible Gift Box, which is now available in the United States on Amazon, the UGREEN Official Store, and TikTok Shop. It contains the Yao Guang-themed collection, and when you open it up, there’s a pop-up effect.

Now, if this does really well, we can hope to see a UGREEN and Zenless Zone Zero collaboration in the future.

items.title: Rin Tohsaka in Honkai Star Rail - Gameplay Revealed for Fate/stay night Character
items.summary: A new crossover heroine is on the way.
items.content:
1 Источник: miHoYo (HoYoverse) 21 Jul 13:53 A new crossover heroine is on the way.

This month, Honkai Star Rail will once again have a collaboration with the Fate/stay night [Unlimited Blade Works] franchise. miHoYo (HoYoverse) has released a gameplay trailer for the new crossover heroine.

The spotlight is on Rin Tohsaka – a five-star rarity character, her path is Erudition, and in combat, she will deal Quantum-type damage. The developers position her as a damage-dealing character who uses gem energy to enhance her skill.

Rin Tohsaka will gain gem energy when allied units consume or restore skill points:

After accumulating enough gem energy, her skill is enhanced. When using the enhanced skill, Rin Tohsaka can convert excess skill points into gem energy. Consuming gem energy additionally deals multiple instances of damage to the selected enemy. When in the same party as Archer, Rin Tohsaka can perform a joint attack and restore a certain amount of skill points to allies.

The Honkai Star Rail x Fate/stay night [Unlimited Blade Works] collaboration is expected to start on July 24. Players will be able to receive a free five-star rarity character of their choice for logging into the game:

items.title: UGREEN powers up with Honkai Star Rail partnership
items.summary: UGREEN has teamed up with Honkai Star Rail in a new partnership that aims to bring together fast charging technology and one of the world's most popular space fantasy games, giving fans in Thailand an opportunity to power up their devices while celebrating their favourite interstellar adventure.
items.content:
UGREEN has teamed up with Honkai Star Rail in a new partnership that aims to bring together fast charging technology and one of the world's most popular space fantasy games, giving fans in Thailand an opportunity to power up their devices while celebrating their favourite interstellar adventure.

Founded in 2012, UGREEN operates in more than 180 countries around the world, while HoYoverse's Honkai Star Rail has grown into one of the most successful role playing games on mobile phones in recent years.

In Honkai Star Rail, players take on the role of a Trailblazer travelling across the galaxy in search of answers about the mysterious Stellaron. Along the way, the protagonist encounters diverse worlds, colourful companions and battles that mix science fiction, mythology and cosmic adventure in equal measure.

The global consumer electronics brand said the collaboration with HoYoverse game company marks its latest push into the gaming sector, building on the success of its previous partnership with Genshin Impact. The move reflects a growing trend among technology companies seeking deeper connections with gaming communities through exclusive products and fan experiences.

At the heart of the partnership is a themed collection inspired by Yao Guang, a military strategist from the Honkai Star Rail game.

The collection will be blending visual identity of Honkai Star Rail with UGREEN's charging technology. The range is designed for gamers, travellers and everyday users who need dependable power on the move. UGREEN said the collection will include charging accessories, 10,000 mAh power banks, smart trackers and retractable USB C charging cables, along with a gift box and exclusive collectible items created for fans of the game. More details are expected to be released next week, ahead of the full launch.

The partnership will also make a high profile appearance at HoYo FEST 2026, which runs from July 30 to Aug 2 at Central Ladprao department store in Bangkok, where the products will be showcased and sold.

Visitors can expect hands-on demonstrations, themed activities and opportunities to interact with fellow fans. Organisers say the exhibition areas have been designed to deliver an immersive experience that celebrates both technology and gaming culture. The events are expected to attract large numbers of players eager to own limited edition products and collectibles.

For fans of the game, the new collaboration offers something practical as well as collectible. After all, saving the galaxy is much easier when your power bank is fully charged.

items.title: Two of PS5's Biggest Games Will Crossover This Winter
items.content:
Zenless Zone Zero will celebrate its second anniversary with v3.1, launching later this month on 29th July.

But while the usual array of free pulls (up to 30 this year) has me feeling happy, it’s confirmation of a crossover event with Honkai: Star Rail that’s got me particularly hyped.

While the exact nature of the collaboration is unknown, it’s expected that the two HoYoverse titles will exchange characters, creating a new kind of cinematic universe.

Honestly, if we get Kafka in ZZZ – which genuinely feels like a good fit – then I’ll explode.

We won’t find out more until later in the year, but for now, v3.1 will plot the arrival of the highly anticipated Remielle Dan, who’ll be available throughout the entire duration of the update.

Sigrid will follow in the second phase, while there’ll be multiple re-runs, including Dialyn, Ukinami Yuzuha, and Asaba Harumasa. Angels of Delusion star Aria will flank Remielle Dan through the first half of the update.

The dev is actually making some changes to the 50/50 in v3.1, allowing you to pick a variety of different agents to obtain if you lose the gacha. This will make team-building a little better, as you’ll hopefully be able to obtain a useful character even when your luck is low.

It’s also giving away a free S-Rank and their W-Engine as part of the second year anniversary celebrations: Jane, Soldier 0 Anby, Hugo, Trigger, and Lucia will all be up for grabs. I’ll probably choose Lucia to help level up her Mindscape.

Speaking of Mindscapes, you’ll be able to further power up Pyrois by engaging in a new permanent event, a dungeon crawler-style mode inspired by the original Hollow exploration gameplay from the title’s launch.

There’ll also be a new seaside-style minigame to celebrate the summer, where you’ll need to comb the beach for seashells that can then be sold at a souvenir shop. I’m really looking forward to that.

Those who participate in the event can unlock a free outfit for Lucy, while Remielle Dan and Sigrid will also be getting swimwear.

That’s a lot of content, but these anniversary updates are always huge. I must say, though, it’s that collaboration with Honkai: Star Rail that I’m really looking forward to – I can’t wait for more details later in the year.

items.title: Hands on with the Honkai: Star Rail v4.0 experience
items.summary: This HoyoVerse train just keeps on chugging!
items.content:
The last article I wrote for Honkai: Star Rail was going over the details of the upcoming Version 4.0 release . Since then, HoYoverse generously offered up the opportunity to play Version 4.0 and experience Planarcadia early. Having spent about a dozen hours or so playing with the new characters, experiencing the story, and generally taking in the sights and sounds of Planarcadia, I’m prepared to share my thoughts on all of it.

It’s best to start off with the main attraction of Version 4.0: Planarcadia. This new planet is a fictional world come to life and boy does it leave a fantastic first impression. Full of the kind of sights and sounds you’d expect from an Asian city brimming with life. Exploration is a blast with all the alleys and little nooks to find, the platforming segments that can either help you navigate across certain parts of the city or find chests within them. There’s also a new style of puzzles that require you to rotate a 2D plane in a 3D space to “paint” certain parts of it to make them solid and let you platform with those objects. Planarcadia is so full of whimsy and still has some of the overhang of a crime family really enabling a super unique feeling planet compared to what we’ve had with previous locations such as the Xianzhou or Amphoreus.

The two new characters following the path of Elation feel great, even if the Aha Moment is a little overstimulating sometimes. Sparxie and Yao Guang fit on a team together like a glove to demolish all the bosses I’ve thrown them against. I’m eager to see where the new path goes with time, especially since we only have the two characters currently. Even as of the live public server branch, we’re unsure when Elation Trailblazer will make their debut, so keep an eye on that as versions progress beyond 4.0. You’ll need to get a hold of new Elation Light Cones as well since they obviously can’t use the other Paths’ options. Burning skill points to increase the Elation stacks until Aha decides he’s satisfied and steps in for a big attack feels really great and it has me curious what future Elation characters might do when they trigger their own Aha Moments.

Not to mention that for new players, you’ve got the opportunity to really kickstart a team of your choice with a new planet. We’re at a point in the game where there’s so many options for characters you can pick from and build around, and 4.0 introducing Elation means that as a new player, you can jump in and get a headstart on pulling for that archetype. Especially with a new Novaflare round of buffs, there’s even more options than before bringing older characters back into the limelight. The Black Swan and Sparkle buffs are so massive that people running DoT teams or any team using an action advance support such as Bronya or Sunday have another insane option. Sparkle was always good, but her Novaflare enables her to further buff your DPS units to extreme levels. Black Swan’s Novaflare was even more insane and rocketed her back up to the top for damage even as a DoT unit. Combined with the option to pick a free character including units like Sparkle, Kafka (who works great in DoT teams), and more, you really can’t go wrong in Planarcadia and the Version 4.0 patch from what I’ve gotten to experience.

Between the free characters, the great change of pace in the story from the latter half of Amphoreus (if you’ve played it, you know), the new cityscape that is quite honestly my new favorite location in the game, and all the new characters to look forward to, you really should make a return to Star Rail if you’ve been away for a minute and are curious about where we’re at. If you’re new, definitely consider giving it a shot. There’s even a bonus to let you convert trace materials for leveling character skills at a 1 to 1 ratio this time instead of a 2 for 1, so you really can kickstart your Elation team. With characters being important again like Silver Wolf, Blade, and Himeko, as well as new faces like Sparxie, Yao Guang, Ashveil, and Pearl, I think 4.0 is just the start of a real treat of a patch cycle for Honkai: Star Rail.

items.title: Honkai: Star Rail 4.0 impressions
items.content:
I'll admit, I recently burned out on Honkai: Star Rail. Upon hearing the news that 3.8 would be the lengthiest patch yet and with content more sparse than ever before, I decided to take a break for the first time since I started playing all the way back in 1.0. The entire Amphoreus saga left me feeling drained; I thoroughly enjoyed 3.0-3.4 as excellent set-up chapters, but felt lost as the plot became a labyrinth of hidden motivations, layered twists, hours of exposition, and endless proper nouns. When looking at the story as a whole, I love the characters (Aglaea and Anaxa in particular) and the rich history of the world within the Scepter, but the later patches often felt like studying for a needlessly convoluted test. I understood the gist of what was happening, but I won't lie to you and say I could describe to you in detail what Cyrene's deal is or how Evernight fits into everything.

All that's to say: I was looking forward to the Astral Express crew taking a well-earned vacation to Planarcadia, the planet of Elation. Despite two of the game's four current versions ostensibly beginning with Akivili's disciples deciding that they need a break from the backbreaking work of saving the universe, neither has resulted in our trailblazers seeing much downtime. Unlike previous excursions, it's all hands on deck: everyone gets off at this stop (save for Black Swan's incredibly hasty and unfortunate exit prior to departure). It's fun to see the entire main cast get involved, none more welcome than Himeko — up until now, the only character trait I could've told you about her was that she's terrible at making coffee. Getting some elaboration on her backstory and giving her a personality outside of "Express mom" is long overdue.

The Trailblazer and March 7th begin their Planarcadian getaway with a suitably devious greeting by none other than our old pal Sparkle. We're given a tour of the vibrant, totally-not-Japanese-inspired streets, crawling with humans living in tentative harmony with totally-not-Penaconian-memetic-entities known as imagenae. Here, we catch a glimpse of what I hope will become one of Planarcadia's core themes: gentrification and the plight of capitalism, with a side of anti-imperialism (all perpetrated by the IPC, of course). It's bold, though, and I worry that future patches will keep these criticisms in the background rather than highlight them as Planarcadia's core issues. I would love so much for little nuggets of lore to be elaborated upon, ones that suggest a more serious political history than Aha would have you believe: what did the imagenae do before the IPC forced them to integrate into society? Were they not allowed to work at all? Were they marginalized and seen as second-class citizens? Is the Department of Aberration Defense an inherently evil organization left to determine on their own which imagenae deserve their continued existence and which do not? Is Kuchiba so hot that it doesn't even matter?

This brings us to the Synwish Syndicate, a gang formerly designed to be a respite for imagenae that feel rejected elsewhere, now, due to IPC intervention, a gang that largely exists to enable monetary gain by questionable means. Here at the syndicate's headquarters, the plot quickly begins to balloon into what feels like a collection of introductions for what's to come. We have Pearl's cold and calculating grip on the planet, Himeko and her confounding family history, the potential return of the Herald of Death, the Stellaron Hunters' unknown involvement in the Phantasmoon Games, Sunday's inadvertent entry into the Games, Evanescia's whole...thing, and Yao Guang's bottomless supply of divinations. What's most shocking is that the patch still manages to pull off a fun (if bloated) self-contained story, adding layers to one of HSR's most inscrutable characters and ending things with one of the game's best low-stakes twists.

I'm no Xianzhou Arbiter-General, but I can pretty easily peer into the future and divine a prophecy for Honkai: Star Rail: each new patch will introduce and/or discuss at length a new prophecy that cryptically divulges the fate of a character and/or planet and/or simulation masquerading as a planet. It's hard to pinpoint the moment a theme becomes a crutch, but I think HSR passed that moment long ago. In this patch, we have not one, not two, but three central characters that are defined by their relationship with prophecy and prediction — Yao Guang, Pearl, and the perennial mystery that is Elio and his script. So many in-game conversations devolve into the interpretation of prophecy, and frankly, it's becoming exhausting. We went through an entire version in which each Chrysos Heir had a poetically vague predetermined fate, and before that (and in 3.8's ret-con-y confusion) we had Sparkle's ambiguous "three deaths" for Firefly.

If this were a wholesale replacement for well-executed foreshadowing, we would have a far bigger issue. It's obvious, though, that the writing team knows how to tell a story sans-prophecy, evident in Sparkle's character-driven narrative in this very patch. Sparxie being the "true" Sparkle (as much as old Sparkle is also the "true" Sparkle) is teased so perfectly; the reveal is obvious in retrospect while being difficult to predict with the given information. It's consistently groan-inducing to see this writing cheat code being used ad nauseam within a story that could function better without it. I hope I'll be proven wrong and the game will subvert the words of its murky prophets, leaving me with egg on my face come the conclusion of this leg of the Express' journey.

4.0 is a promising new beginning, and has at the very least brought me back in terms of its story. Though there is an abundance of new proper nouns to memorize and a bevy of new and returning cast members to keep track of, the patch still takes on a welcome return to simplicity and levity (save for the shocking tonal shift right at the end, RIP the Synwish Syndicate Feb. 2026 - Feb. 2026). As long as things don't get too bogged down in extensive metaphors and tiresome exposition, I'm hopeful that Planarcadia can become the most focused version of HSR we've seen so far.
